purpose: Generates a procedural spider web spline from N anchor points read from an OM Null's children. Output: single multi-segment Nodes Spline containing radial threads + concentric spiral rings.
visual_result: Top-down: 6 radial threads from boundary anchors converging at a center, with concentric spiral rings between them. Classic spider-web silhouette.
input_geometry: OM Null whose children are the boundary anchor positions (variable count, data-driven).

doc_level_objects.name: Spline
doc_level_objects.role: boundary spline reference (possibly visual aid only)

annotation_storage_pattern: scaffold@ section labels (Spiral Thread / Single Thread calculation / All Threads / Randomize) — same pattern as scene 03.

am_sliders.name: Use Null Obj
am_sliders.controls: toggle whether to use bound Null's children as anchors

am_sliders.name: Null Obj
am_sliders.controls: OM-bound Null whose children are the anchor positions

am_sliders.name: Input × 9
am_sliders.controls: per-section tunable params consumed inside the 4 scaffold groups

am_exposure_pattern_note: ALL parameters surface as root.in@<hash> direct ports — ZERO floatingio wrappers. The 9 generic 'Input' entries make the AM less ergonomic; modern scenes should add floatingio for labels. This is the same root-direct pattern observed in scene 04.

key_connections.purpose: Variable-count anchor array — replaces N separate legacyobjectaccess nodes.
Per-segment point-count array — defines spline topology.
Flat list of all points across all segments — concatenated radials + spiral.
Final spline geometry → root spline output template.
Normalized [0,1] parameter for curve-falloff lookup along thread.
OM-bound Null parameter (Null Obj root input).

output_routing.via: assembler.Geometry → Bezier Spline Assembler.geometryout → host spline output
output_routing.host_visibility_path: Nodes Spline (180420700) renders root spline output as classic LineObject in OM
output_routing.notes: Multi-segment spline output via Points + Segments parallel arrays. THE canonical Nodes-Spline output pattern.

interesting_types: Variable-count anchor array via children@ (vs hardcoded legacyobjectaccess per anchor)
Multi-segment spline topology via Points[] + Segments[] parallel arrays
Spline-typed AM port (Spline Mapper) for per-thread shape curve
9 generic root-direct AM Inputs — exposure without floatingio labels

core_idea_nodes.name: Children Op
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: OM-children enumeration primitive. Reads N children of an OM-bound Null as an array — replaces N separate legacyobjectaccess nodes for data-driven variable-count input.

core_idea_nodes.name: Assemble Spline
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: THE multi-segment spline output primitive. Takes parallel Points + Segments arrays and reconstructs spline topology. Without this you'd need N separate Nodes Spline hosts for N segments.

core_idea_nodes.name: Spline Mapper
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Spline-typed AM curve editor — artist authors per-thread shape curve. Same pattern as scene 03's Bevel Profil. Demonstrates the Spline-typed AM port surface.

core_idea_nodes.name: Append Elements (×3)
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Three parallel growth points: per-thread points, per-segment count, cross-segment combine. Demonstrates the multi-array bookkeeping pattern for assembler input.

core_idea_nodes.name: Iterate Collection (×4)
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Multi-level nested iteration: anchor pairs × points-per-thread × spiral rings × points-per-ring. Complete topology for the radial+spiral structure.

scaffolding_nodes: scaffold × 4 (section labels: Spiral Thread / Single Thread calculation / All Threads / Randomize)
what_is_clever: Multi-segment spline assembly via Points + Segments parallel arrays — packs N independent threads into ONE artist-friendly Nodes-Spline output.
children@ for variable-count input — anchor count is data-driven by OM children, no hardcoded legacyobjectaccess fan-out.
Concatenate + parallel Append for topology bookkeeping — radials + spiral stay synchronized in two parallel arrays.
Spline Mapper for per-thread shape modulation — artist-authored curve drives thread shape without graph changes.
No memory@ — pure procedural. Rebuilds entirely each frame from inputs; no state, no sequential-stepping requirement.

gotchas.issue: Plugin ID 180420700 ('Nodes Spline') uses Neutron nodespace HERE but used the older nodespace.scene in scene 02. Plugin ID alone is NOT sufficient to determine nodespace.
gotchas.evidence: host.GetAllNimbusRefs() on this scene's Nodes Spline returns net.maxon.neutron.nodespace (NOT scene). Different from scene 02's same-ID host.
gotchas.workaround: ALWAYS probe host.GetAllNimbusRefs() for actual nodespace. Update gotcha #56 wording — table is 'common case' not authoritative.

gotchas.issue: 9 root-direct AM ports labeled 'Input' (generic) — reduces AM UX clarity.
gotchas.evidence: root.GetInputs() walked: Op, Use Null Obj, Null Obj, Loop Count, Loop Start, Loop End, random, Children, Input × 9.
gotchas.workaround: When recreating, prefer floatingio wrappers with semantic effectivename labels.

rebuild_recipe: 1. Create Nodes Spline (180420700) host. Add OM-bound Null parameter.
2. Inside the Neutron graph: add children@ node, bind Children Op input to root's Null Obj parameter.
3. Use getcount@ to determine N (anchor count).
4. Build a range@ with end ← N to drive radials iteration.
5. containeriteration over the children Array — for each anchor pair (i, i+1):
a. Lerp between anchor[i] and anchor[i+1] (or anchor[i] and center) for thread interpolation.
b. Apply Spline Mapper for shape modulation.
c. Append each interpolated position to global Points array.
d. Append the thread's point-count to Segments array.
6. Build a separate spiral generator: nested containeriteration over [0..LoopCount] rings × [0..points_per_ring] points.
- Each ring at radius lerp(LoopStart, LoopEnd, ring_idx/LoopCount).
- Each point at angle θ = 2π * pt_idx / N.
7. Concatenate radials + spiral into a single flat Points array.
8. Assembler (Assemble Spline) consumes Segments + Points → emits Geometry → root spline output.
9. Optional: hash@ of point-index + 'random' slider drives per-vertex jitter.
10. Expose 9 root-direct AM ports: Use Null Obj, Null Obj, Loop Count/Start/End, random, Children + 3 generic Inputs.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: multi_segment_spline_assembler
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Pack N independent spline segments into a single artist-friendly Nodes-Spline output via parallel Points + Segments arrays.
nodes: append (Points array — accumulates all points across all segments)
concat (optional — combines multiple Points arrays from different generators)
assembler (Segments + Points in → Geometry out → root spline)
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Generalizes far beyond spider webs: ivy across surfaces, network diagrams as one spline, mesh-edge skeleton, path bundles, light streaks, hair tufts. ANY scene where N independent splines need to be packed into ONE Nodes-Spline output instead of N separate hosts.
secondary_recipes: R13_om_children_enumeration — children@ for variable-count OM input
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: children@ node creation not yet exposed via cinema4d-mcp scene_nodes_add_node
assembler@ Points+Segments parallel-array wiring needs documentation
Spline-typed AM port surface not yet synthesizable

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: Plugin ID 180420700 can host EITHER nodespace. Update gotcha #56 — IDs are 'common case' not authoritative.
children@ is the canonical OM-children-iteration primitive. Recipe-worthy.
assembler@ is THE multi-segment spline output primitive. Points + Segments parallel-array pattern is load-bearing.
Generic 'Input' root-direct ports are a UX anti-pattern; prefer floatingio with effectivename labels.
No memory@ = no sequential-stepping requirement. Detect pure-procedural vs simulation by checking for memory@ presence.
